title: The Red Umbrella
introKo:
비 오는 날, 당신의 이야기.
선택에 따라 장면이 달라져요.

quiz.q: 이야기 속 할아버지가 나에게 준 것은 무엇인가요?

easy.text: It rains. You run to the bus stop. You have no umbrella.
Your coat is wet. Your shoes are wet. The bus comes in ten minutes.

options.reaction: You wait under the roof. The wind is cold. You are cold.
There is no taxi today. You put your phone away. You sigh.

easy.text: An old man comes. He has a big red umbrella.
He smiles. He holds the umbrella over you.

options.reaction: "It is nothing," he says. "Rain is hard with no umbrella."
"Don't worry," he says. "There is room for two."

easy.text: You wait for the bus together. You talk about the rain. He talks about his garden.
The bus comes. The man gives you the umbrella.
"Please keep it," he says. "Someone gave it to me, too."

options.en: I will remember this. Thank you!
options.reaction: He waves his hand. The bus goes. You hold the umbrella.

options.en: Then I will give it to someone, too.
options.reaction: "Good!" he says with a big smile. The bus door closes.

easy.text: You smile all the way home. The red umbrella is by your door. It waits for the next rainy day.

normal.text: Rain falls on the small town. You run to the bus stop, but you have no umbrella.
Your coat and shoes are wet. The bus is ten minutes away.

options.reaction: You stand under the roof, but the wind blows the rain at you. You are cold.

options.en: Check your phone for a taxi.
options.reaction: No taxis come on rainy days. You put your phone back and sigh.

normal.text: Then an old man walks to the bus stop. He has a big red umbrella.
He looks at you and smiles. He holds the umbrella over your head.

options.reaction: "It's nothing," he says. "Rainy days are hard without an umbrella."
"Don't worry," he laughs. "There is room for two under here."

normal.text: You wait for the bus together and talk about the rain. The old man tells you about his little garden.
The bus comes. The man gives the umbrella to you.
"Please keep it," he says. "Someone gave it to me on a rainy day, too."

options.en: I will remember this. Thank you!
options.reaction: He waves his hand as the bus leaves. You hold the red umbrella close.

options.en: Then I will give it to someone, too.
options.reaction: "That's the spirit!" he says with a big smile. The bus door closes.

normal.text: You smile all the way home. The red umbrella stands by your door, waiting for the next rainy day.

hard.text: Cold rain pours down on the small town as you hurry toward the bus stop, and of course, today of all days, you have no umbrella.
Your coat is soaked through and your shoes squelch with every step. The next bus is still ten minutes away.

options.en: Take shelter under the narrow roof.
options.reaction: You press yourself under the narrow roof, but the wind keeps driving the rain sideways into you. You are shivering before long.

options.en: Check your phone for a taxi.
options.reaction: Taxis never seem to exist on rainy days. You slip the phone back into your pocket with a sigh.

hard.text: Then an elderly man strolls up to the bus stop, carrying a splendid red umbrella.
He glances at you, smiles knowingly, and without a word tilts the umbrella over your head.

options.reaction: "It's nothing," he chuckles. "Rainy days are unkind to people without umbrellas."

options.en: Oh, you really don't have to!
options.reaction: "Nonsense," he laughs. "There is plenty of room for two under here."

hard.text: The two of you wait for the bus together, trading small talk about the weather. He tells you about the little garden he tends behind his house.
When the bus finally arrives, the man presses the umbrella into your hands.
"Please keep it," he says. "Someone once handed it to me on a rainy day, just like this."

options.en: I will remember this. Thank you!
options.reaction: He waves from the curb as the bus pulls away. You hold the red umbrella a little closer.

options.en: Then I will pass it on to someone, too.
options.reaction: "That's the spirit!" he beams as the doors slide shut.

hard.text: You smile the whole way home. The red umbrella now stands by your door, waiting patiently for the next rainy day.
